binaries.title: ksecdd.sys (Windows Kernel-Mode SSPI Security Device Driver) - NULL-pointer dereference and missing ProbeForRead hardening in SSPI dispatch and fast-I/O IOCTL handlers

changed_functions.name: EncryptMessage (SSPI SealMessage)
key_changes: Added NULL check on phContext (arg1) and on the message-buffer argument; unpatched dereferenced *phContext without checking phContext
Added lfence after the unsigned upper-bound check on the package index (speculative bounds-check-bypass barrier)
Reloaded the dispatch-table base pointer after the bounds check
Added NULL check on the resolved package entry pointer and on the function pointer at offset 0x30 before the CFG-guarded indirect call
changed_functions.security_notes: Unpatched EncryptMessage reads *phContext (package index) with no NULL check on phContext; if phContext is NULL the load faults in kernel mode (local BSOD). The dispatch then does an unsigned upper-bound check and loads the package entry and function pointer without NULL-checking either; the indirect call is via __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr (Control-Flow-Guard), not a retpoline. The patch adds NULL checks on phContext/message, an lfence, a table-base reload, and entry/function-pointer NULL checks. Impact is a local NULL-pointer dereference DoS; no OOB or code-execution primitive is demonstrable (the upper-bound check is present in both builds).
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED EncryptMessage @ 0x1C00262D0 (vulnerable block):
0x1C002631D  mov rdx, [rbx]                 ; rdx = *phContext (rbx=phContext); NO NULL check
0x1C0026320  mov ecx, [rax+1D0h]            ; entry count
0x1C0026326  cmp rdx, rcx
0x1C0026329  jnb loc_1C0026377             ; unsigned upper bound only; no lfence
0x1C002632B  mov rax, [r8+rdx*8]           ; entry = table[index]; NO NULL check
0x1C002633B  mov rax, [rax+30h]            ; fn ptr (offset 0x30); NO NULL check
0x1C002633F  call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

PATCHED EncryptMessage @ 0x180029A70 (fixed block):
0x180029A80  test rcx, rcx                 ; NEW: NULL check phContext
0x180029A83  jnz loc_180029A93
0x180029A85  mov eax, 80090301h            ; SEC_E_INVALID_HANDLE
0x180029A93  test rbx, rbx                 ; NEW: NULL check message arg
0x180029A96  jz  loc_180029A85
0x180029AE3  lfence                        ; NEW: speculation barrier
0x180029AE6  mov rax, [rcx+1C8h]           ; NEW: reload table base after check
0x180029AED  mov rcx, [rax+rbp*8]          ; entry = table[index]
0x180029AF1  test rcx, rcx                 ; NEW: NULL check entry
0x180029AF4  jz  loc_180029B45
0x180029AF6  mov rax, [rcx+30h]            ; fn ptr (offset 0x30)
0x180029AFA  test rax, rax                 ; NEW: NULL check fn ptr
0x180029AFD  jz  loc_180029B30
0x180029B0A  call _guard_dispatch_icall

changed_functions.name: DecryptMessage (SSPI UnsealMessage)
key_changes: Added NULL checks on phContext and the message argument
Added lfence + dispatch-table reload after the bounds check
Added NULL checks on the package entry pointer and the function pointer at offset 0x38
changed_functions.security_notes: Same pattern as EncryptMessage; function-pointer offset 0x38. Unpatched at 0x1C002625E reads *phContext without a NULL check on phContext; patched adds NULL checks, lfence, table reload, and entry/fn-ptr NULL checks. Local NULL-deref DoS.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED DecryptMessage @ 0x1C0026210:
0x1C002625E  mov rdx, [rbx]     ; *phContext; NO NULL check
0x1C0026261  mov ecx, [rax+1D0h]
0x1C0026267  cmp rdx, rcx
0x1C002626A  jnb loc_1C00262B9  ; upper bound only
0x1C002626C  mov rax, [r8+rdx*8]; entry; NO NULL check
0x1C002627D  mov rax, [rax+38h] ; fn ptr (0x38); NO NULL check
0x1C0026281  call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

PATCHED DecryptMessage @ 0x180029D00:
0x180029D11  test rcx, rcx      ; NEW: NULL check phContext
0x180029D14  jz loc_180029DBC
0x180029D1A  test rdx, rdx      ; NEW: NULL check message arg
0x180029D1D  jz loc_180029DBC
0x180029D6E  lfence             ; NEW
0x180029D71  mov rax, [rcx+1C8h]; NEW: reload table base
0x180029D78  mov rcx, [rax+rbp*8]
0x180029D7C  test rcx, rcx      ; NEW: NULL check entry
0x180029D81  mov rax, [rcx+38h] ; fn ptr (0x38)
0x180029D85  test rax, rax      ; NEW: NULL check fn ptr
0x180029D96  call _guard_dispatch_icall

changed_functions.name: QuerySecurityContextToken
key_changes: Added NULL check on phContext

Moved the inline dispatch-table indexing into the validating helper KernelPackageCallValidatorWorkers::CallValidator<...>::InvokeById at 0x180029c10 (offset-0x40 slot), which performs the bounds check, lfence, table reload, entry-NULL and function-pointer-NULL checks
changed_functions.security_notes: Unpatched reads *phContext as the package index with no NULL check, upper-bound checks it, then loads the entry and the function pointer at offset 0x40 and calls via __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr without NULL checks. On success the returned token handle is duplicated via NtDuplicateObject. Patched adds the phContext NULL check and delegates the dispatch to the validating InvokeById helper.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED QuerySecurityContextToken @ 0x1C0026010:
0x1C002605C  mov rcx, [rbx]     ; *phContext; NO NULL check
0x1C002605F  mov eax, [rax+1D0h]
0x1C0026065  cmp rcx, rax
0x1C0026068  jnb loc_1C00260D8  ; upper bound only
0x1C002606A  mov rax, [rdx+rcx*8]; entry; NO NULL check
0x1C002607A  mov rax, [rax+40h] ; fn ptr (0x40); NO NULL check
0x1C002607E  call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

PATCHED InvokeById @ 0x180029C10 (offset-0x40 slot):
0x180029C6B  lfence
0x180029C6E  mov rcx, [rax+1C8h] ; reload table base
0x180029C75  mov rax, [rcx+rbx*8]; entry
0x180029C79  test rax, rax       ; NULL check entry
0x180029C7E  mov rax, [rax+40h]  ; fn ptr (0x40)
0x180029C82  test rax, rax       ; NULL check fn ptr
0x180029C90  call _guard_dispatch_icall

changed_functions.name: ImpersonateSecurityContext
key_changes: Added NULL check on phContext (returns 0xC000000D)
Replaced inline dispatch-table indexing with the validating InvokeById helper at 0x180029c10 (offset-0x40 slot)
changed_functions.security_notes: Same dispatch pattern; unpatched reads *phContext without a NULL check and calls the offset-0x40 function pointer without entry/fn-ptr NULL checks (at 0x1C00263D8 / 0x1C00263F5). On success the function calls PsImpersonateClient with the resulting token. Patched adds the phContext NULL check and delegates the dispatch to InvokeById.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED ImpersonateSecurityContext @ 0x1C0026390:
0x1C00263D8  mov rdx, [rbx]     ; *phContext; NO NULL check
0x1C00263E1  cmp rdx, rcx       ; upper bound only
0x1C00263E6  mov rax, [r8+rdx*8]; entry; NO NULL check
0x1C00263F5  mov rax, [rax+40h] ; fn ptr (0x40); NO NULL check
0x1C00263F9  call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

PATCHED ImpersonateSecurityContext @ 0x180029B60:
0x180029B6D  test rcx, rcx      ; NEW: NULL check phContext
0x180029B70  jnz loc_180029BA8
0x180029BA1  mov eax, 0C000000Dh
0x180029BB7  call InvokeById (0x180029C10)

changed_functions.name: DeleteUserModeContext (sub_1c001e190)
key_changes: Dispatch moved into the validating InvokeById helper at 0x180029960 (offset-0x08 slot), gaining lfence, table reload, and package-entry NULL check
changed_functions.security_notes: Correction to the systemic claim: the unpatched DeleteUserModeContext ALREADY NULL-checks its context-handle argument (test rbx,rbx / jz at 0x1C001E1DB) and the function pointer (test rax,rax / jz at 0x1C001E1F6). For this function the added protection is the lfence, the dispatch-table reload, and the entry-pointer NULL check (offset-0x08 slot), delivered by the shared InvokeById helper.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED DeleteUserModeContext @ 0x1C001E190 (already guarded):
0x1C001E1DB  test rbx, rbx      ; existing NULL check on context handle
0x1C001E1DE  jz  loc_1C001E251
0x1C001E1E0  mov rcx, [rbx]     ; *arg (index)
0x1C001E1E9  cmp rcx, rax       ; upper bound
0x1C001E1EE  mov rax, [rdx+rcx*8]; entry (no entry-NULL check in unpatched)
0x1C001E1F2  mov rax, [rax+8]   ; fn ptr (offset 0x08)
0x1C001E1F6  test rax, rax      ; existing NULL check on fn ptr
0x1C001E1F9  jz  loc_1C001E24A
0x1C001E203  call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

changed_functions.name: ProcessSecurityContextUnMarshalResponse (sub_1c001fac0)
key_changes: Same dispatch hardening (lfence / entry-NULL / fn-ptr-NULL) at the offset-0x10 dispatch site
changed_functions.security_notes: Correction to the report's naming: this is ProcessSecurityContextUnMarshalResponse, an async-response unmarshaller, not a QueryContextAttributes/SetContextAttributes dispatcher. Its dispatch index (0x1C001FC7B, offset-0x10 function pointer) is taken from previously unmarshalled response data rather than a raw caller-supplied context handle; it received the same dispatch hardening.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED ProcessSecurityContextUnMarshalResponse @ 0x1C001FAC0 (dispatch site):
0x1C001FC7B  mov rcx, [rdi]     ; index from unmarshalled data
0x1C001FC84  cmp rcx, rax       ; upper bound only
0x1C001FC8D  mov rax, [rdx+rcx*8]; entry; NO NULL check
0x1C001FC9F  mov rax, [rax+10h] ; fn ptr (offset 0x10); NO NULL check
0x1C001FCA3  call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

changed_functions.name: SspiAcceptSecurityContextAsync
key_changes: Added bound check: if (pOutput->cBuffers > 0x12) return 0x8009035D (SEC_E_INVALID_TOKEN)
Added NULL checks on phContext/phCredential/phNewContext and a MapKernelContextHandle call; function restructured (unpatched delegated directly to KsecProcessSecurityContext)
changed_functions.security_notes: The added cBuffers <= 0x12 check is a defensive upper bound on the caller-supplied output SecBufferDesc; pOutput is not network-attacker data and no overflow is demonstrable in the unpatched build. The internal per-package caps in KsecProcessSecurityContext/SspipProcessSecurityContext were harmonized to 0x12 (previously 8 / 0xA), so this is a defensive normalization rather than a fix for a reachable overflow. Severity low. Correction: the error code is 0x8009035D (SEC_E_INVALID_TOKEN), not SEC_E_INVALID_HANDLE, and the check is at 0x1800241E4, not 0x1800242bc.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
PATCHED @ 0x1800241E4:  cmp dword ptr [r15+4], 12h   ; pOutput->cBuffers (offset +4)
0x1800241E9:  ja loc_1800242BC              ; -> return 0x8009035D
UNPATCHED: no such check; delegates to KsecProcessSecurityContext

changed_functions.name: ImportSecurityContextW
key_changes: Added NULL checks: if (pPackedContext == NULL || phContext == NULL) return STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xC000000D)
changed_functions.security_notes: Unpatched dereferences the output handle phContext (phContext->dwUpper = ...; phContext->dwLower = ...) without a NULL check; patched adds NULL checks on pPackedContext and phContext. Both builds already NULL-check the package function pointer (if v10 != NULL), so no function-pointer check was added. Impact: local NULL-deref DoS if a caller passes NULL phContext.

changed_functions.name: KsecIoctlHandleFunctionReturn
key_changes: Unpatched forwarded the raw user pointer to CallInProgressCompleted after only a force-fault touch of the range end; patched adds ProbeForRead + RtlCopyFromUser into a pool buffer and operates on the kernel copy (double-fetch / TOCTOU + missing-probe fix)
changed_functions.security_notes: Strongest member of the fast-I/O IOCTL probing family (see security_findings). Unpatched: if (a1+size > MmUserProbeAddress || overflow) *(BYTE*)MmUserProbeAddress = 0; then CallInProgressCompleted(a1, a1, size) on the raw user pointer. Patched: ProbeForRead(Src, size, 1) at 0x1800067BE, ExAllocatePool2, RtlCopyFromUser(Pool, Src, size), CallInProgressCompleted(Pool, Pool, size).

changed_functions.name: KsecIoctl* fast-I/O handler family (KsecIoctlClientCallback, KsecIoctlAllocVm, KsecIoctlFreeVm, KsecIoctlCopyVm, KsecIoctlAllocPool, KsecIoctlFreePool, KsecIoctlCopyPool, KsecIoctlDupLsaHandle, KsecIoctlInsertProtectedProcessAddress, KsecIoctlRemoveProtectedProcessAddress, KsecCopyVirtualMemory)
key_changes: Added ProbeForRead on the caller-supplied input buffer before dereference; some replaced a weak start-of-range test (if *a1 >= MmUserProbeAddress) with a full ProbeForRead; KsecIoctlClientCallback also captures fields into kernel locals
The unpatched binary contains zero ProbeForRead calls; the patched binary contains 24
changed_functions.security_notes: See security_findings entry 5. This handler family, reached through the fast-I/O device-control path, is the genuine IOCTL-path hardening. The handle values are separately validated via ObReferenceObjectByHandle(PsProcessType) in both builds and addresses via KsecRemoveValidVm/KsecInsertValidVm; the added probes close the missing user-mode-range validation.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
PATCHED KsecIoctlFreeVm @ 0x18000E168:
0x18000E196  call cs:__imp_ProbeForRead   ; Length 0x10, Alignment 8 (NEW)
0x18000E1A2  movups xmm0, [rbx]           ; deref of caller buffer AFTER probe
UNPATCHED KsecIoctlFreeVm @ 0x1C000E91C: no ProbeForRead; reads *a1 then ObReferenceObjectByHandle at 0x1C000E963

changed_functions.name: KsecDispatch (sub_1c001e8c0)
key_changes: IOCTL 0x398000 handler present and functionally identical in both builds (opens a handle to the caller's own current process, stores it in the kernel LSA state, returns only the DWORD KsecSystemProcessId to the user buffer)
IOCTL 0x390058 handler functionally identical in both builds (METHOD_BUFFERED; validates the target via KsecRemoveValidVm then ZwFreeVirtualMemory)
Diff is refactoring: InitSecurityInterfaceW -> CreateClient, extension-dispatch signature, IoctlIpcGetQueuedFunctionCalls
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. The 0x398000 and 0x390058 IOCTL handlers are unchanged between builds. 0x478 in the 0x398000 branch is a set of PROCESS_* access rights on the caller's own process (not TOKEN_* rights) and the object type argument is NULL; the resulting handle is stored in the kernel LSA state, not returned to user mode. The 0x390058 branch is METHOD_BUFFERED and validated via KsecRemoveValidVm in both builds. The ProbeForRead/ObReferenceObjectByHandle additions are in the separate KsecIoctl* fast-I/O handlers, not in KsecDispatch.

changed_functions.name: AcceptSecurityContext
key_changes: Thin wrapper updated to call the restructured worker (sub_1800248d0)
changed_functions.security_notes: Wrapper delegation change; no behavioral security impact.

changed_functions.name: KsecProcessSecurityContext (sub_1c001e260)
key_changes: Token-duplication and process-token-query paths refactored; internal helper renumbering (sub_1c0003610 -> sub_1800011d0)
Per-package output cBuffers cap changed 8 -> 0x12 (loosened, not tightened)
changed_functions.security_notes: Refactor of server-side token processing. The internal cBuffers cap was loosened to 0x12, consistent with the harmonized limit in SspiAcceptSecurityContextAsync; not a security tightening.

added: KsecUpdateUserTokenSessionId (new fast-I/O handler present only in the patched build; part of the same ProbeForRead hardening wave)

security_findings.function: EncryptMessage / DecryptMessage / QuerySecurityContextToken / ImpersonateSecurityContext / DeleteUserModeContext / ProcessSecurityContextUnMarshalResponse
security_findings.entry_point: Kernel SSP message/context routines (EncryptMessage=SealMessage, DecryptMessage=UnsealMessage, QuerySecurityContextToken, ImpersonateSecurityContext) invoked with a caller-supplied PCtxtHandle
security_findings.vulnerability_class: CWE-476 (NULL Pointer Dereference); added lfence speculation barrier on the dispatch index
security_findings.description: In the unpatched build, EncryptMessage, DecryptMessage, QuerySecurityContextToken, and ImpersonateSecurityContext read the package index from *phContext without first checking phContext for NULL, then index a per-package dispatch table (unsigned upper-bound check only) and call through a function pointer via the Control-Flow-Guard dispatch thunk without NULL-checking the resolved entry or function pointer. If phContext is NULL, the load of *phContext faults in kernel mode (local BSOD). The patch adds NULL checks on phContext (and the message-buffer argument for Encrypt/Decrypt), an lfence after the bounds check, a reload of the dispatch-table base, and NULL checks on the package entry and function pointer. QuerySecurityContextToken/ImpersonateSecurityContext/DeleteUserModeContext route the dispatch through shared validating helpers (CallValidator::InvokeById at 0x180029c10 and 0x180029960). Note: DeleteUserModeContext already NULL-checked its handle and function pointer in the unpatched build.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Invoke EncryptMessage/DecryptMessage/QuerySecurityContextToken/ImpersonateSecurityContext with a NULL context handle. Unpatched faults on the *phContext load; patched returns SEC_E_INVALID_HANDLE (0x80090301).
security_findings.primitive: Local kernel NULL-pointer dereference -> BSOD (denial of service). No out-of-bounds read/write or code-execution primitive is demonstrable: the upper-bound check on the index is present in both builds, and the added lfence is a speculative bounds-check-bypass barrier.
security_findings.evidence: See changed_functions entries for EncryptMessage/DecryptMessage/QuerySecurityContextToken/ImpersonateSecurityContext for the real disassembly. Indirect call is cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr (CFG), not a retpoline.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED EncryptMessage @ 0x1C00262D0:
0x1C002631D  mov rdx, [rbx]      ; *phContext; NO NULL check
0x1C0026329  jnb error          ; unsigned upper bound only
0x1C002632B  mov rax, [r8+rdx*8] ; entry; NO NULL check
0x1C002633B  mov rax, [rax+30h]  ; fn ptr; NO NULL check
0x1C002633F  call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

PATCHED EncryptMessage @ 0x180029A70:
0x180029A80  test rcx, rcx       ; NEW NULL check
0x180029AE3  lfence              ; NEW
0x180029AE6  mov rax, [rcx+1C8h] ; NEW reload
0x180029AF1  test rcx, rcx       ; NEW entry NULL check
0x180029AFA  test rax, rax       ; NEW fn-ptr NULL check

security_findings.entry_point: DeviceIoControl to the ksecdd device - IOCTL codes 0x398000 and 0x390058
security_findings.description: No security-relevant change. Both IOCTL handlers are functionally identical in the unpatched and patched builds. IOCTL 0x398000 opens a handle to PsGetCurrentProcess() (the caller's own process) with ObOpenObjectByPointer(process, OBJ_KERNEL_HANDLE, NULL, 0x478, NULL, KernelMode, &state[1]); the handle is stored into the kernel LSA-state structure, not returned to user mode, and the user output buffer receives only the DWORD KsecSystemProcessId. 0x478 is a set of PROCESS_* rights (PROCESS_VM_OPERATION | PROCESS_VM_READ | PROCESS_VM_WRITE | PROCESS_DUP_HANDLE | PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION) on the caller's own process, and the object-type argument is NULL. The handler is NOT removed in the patched build (present at 0x180026dc0, ObOpenObjectByPointer with 0x478). IOCTL 0x390058 is METHOD_BUFFERED, requires RequestorMode == UserMode, and validates the target address against ksecdd's own tracked-allocation list via KsecRemoveValidVm before ZwFreeVirtualMemory; no ProbeForRead or ObReferenceObjectByHandle was added to this branch. The only diff in this function is refactoring (InitSecurityInterfaceW -> CreateClient, extension dispatch).
security_findings.evidence: UNPATCHED KsecDispatch 0x398000 branch: ObOpenObjectByPointer(*v19, 0x200u, nullptr, 0x478u, nullptr, 0, v19 + 1); *(_DWORD*)&MappedSystemVa->Type = KsecSystemProcessId; v30 = 4. PATCHED KsecDispatch 0x398000 branch (0x180026dc0): Client = ObOpenObjectByPointer(*v25, 0x200u, nullptr, 0x478u, nullptr, 0, v25 + 1); *a3 = KsecSystemProcessId; v32 = 4. Both identical.

security_findings.vulnerability_class: CWE-1284 (Improper Validation of Specified Quantity) - defensive bound
security_findings.description: The patched SspiAcceptSecurityContextAsync adds if (pOutput->cBuffers > 0x12) return 0x8009035D (SEC_E_INVALID_TOKEN), plus NULL checks on phContext/phCredential/phNewContext and a MapKernelContextHandle call. pOutput is the caller-supplied output SecBufferDesc, not network-attacker-controlled data, and no overflow is demonstrable in the unpatched build. The internal per-package caps in KsecProcessSecurityContext/SspipProcessSecurityContext were harmonized to 0x12 (from 8 / 0xA), so this is a defensive normalization.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Call SspiAcceptSecurityContextAsync with a caller-supplied pOutput whose cBuffers exceeds 0x12; patched returns SEC_E_INVALID_TOKEN.
security_findings.primitive: Defensive upper bound on a caller-provided buffer count; no demonstrable overflow primitive.
security_findings.evidence: PATCHED @ 0x1800241E4: cmp dword ptr [r15+4], 12h ; ja 0x1800242BC. SecBufferDesc.cBuffers at offset +4. UNPATCHED: no such check.

security_findings.description: The unpatched ImportSecurityContextW dereferences the output context handle phContext (phContext->dwUpper = ...; phContext->dwLower = ...) without a NULL check. The patch adds if (pPackedContext == NULL || phContext == NULL) return STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xC000000D). Both builds already NULL-check the package function pointer, so no function-pointer check was added.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Call ImportSecurityContextW with a NULL phContext (or NULL pPackedContext).
security_findings.evidence: UNPATCHED: v10 = *(v13 + 96); if (v10 != nullptr) { v8 = v10(pPackedContext, Token, &v14); if (v8 >= 0) { phContext->dwUpper = v14; phContext->dwLower = v15; } }  (phContext dereferenced without NULL check). PATCHED: if (pPackedContext != nullptr && phContext != nullptr) { ... } else return 0xC000000D.

security_findings.function: KsecIoctl* fast-I/O IOCTL handlers (KsecIoctlHandleFunctionReturn, KsecIoctlClientCallback, KsecIoctlAllocVm, KsecIoctlFreeVm, KsecIoctlCopyVm, KsecIoctlAllocPool, KsecIoctlFreePool, KsecIoctlCopyPool, KsecIoctlDupLsaHandle, KsecIoctlInsertProtectedProcessAddress, KsecIoctlRemoveProtectedProcessAddress, KsecCopyVirtualMemory)
security_findings.entry_point: ksecdd fast-I/O device control (KsecFastIoDeviceControl 0x1c0002910) dispatching CNG kernel IOCTL codes to the KsecIoctl* handlers
security_findings.vulnerability_class: CWE-822 (Untrusted Pointer Dereference) / missing ProbeForRead; CWE-367 (double-fetch/TOCTOU) for KsecIoctlHandleFunctionReturn and KsecIoctlClientCallback
security_findings.description: The fast-I/O device-control path passes the caller-supplied input-buffer pointer straight to the per-IOCTL handlers. In the unpatched build these handlers dereference that pointer with no ProbeForRead (the entire unpatched binary contains zero ProbeForRead calls; the patched binary contains 24). The patch adds ProbeForRead before the dereference across the whole KsecIoctl* handler family. Two handlers get a stronger fix: KsecIoctlHandleFunctionReturn (unpatched only force-fault-touched the range end and forwarded the raw user pointer to CallInProgressCompleted; patched adds ProbeForRead + RtlCopyFromUser into a pool buffer and operates on the kernel copy) and KsecIoctlClientCallback (multiple ProbeForRead + capture of fields into kernel locals). Some handlers previously used only a weak start-of-range test (if *a1 >= MmUserProbeAddress) replaced by a full ProbeForRead. This is the genuine IOCTL-path hardening; the report's earlier 0x398000/0x390058 framing (see finding 2) does not correspond to any change.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Reach the ksecdd fast-I/O device-control interface with one of the CNG kernel IOCTL codes and an input-buffer pointer that is not a valid user-mode range (or that is mutated between fetches, for the double-fetch handlers). Reachability by an unprivileged caller depends on the device ACL and the protected-process gating in KsecFastIoDeviceControl.
security_findings.primitive: Kernel-mode dereference of an unvalidated caller-supplied pointer (invalid-address fault, or bounded read from a caller-named address). Handle values read from the buffer are separately validated via ObReferenceObjectByHandle(PsProcessType) and addresses via KsecRemoveValidVm/KsecInsertValidVm, which bounds the practical impact.
security_findings.evidence:
PATCHED KsecIoctlFreeVm @ 0x18000E168: 0x18000E196 call cs:__imp_ProbeForRead (len 0x10, align 8) then 0x18000E1A2 movups xmm0,[rbx]. UNPATCHED KsecIoctlFreeVm @ 0x1C000E91C: no ProbeForRead; reads *a1 directly then ObReferenceObjectByHandle at 0x1C000E963.

PATCHED KsecIoctlHandleFunctionReturn @ 0x180006728: ProbeForRead(Src, size, 1) at 0x1800067BE; ExAllocatePool2; RtlCopyFromUser(Pool, Src, size); CallInProgressCompleted(Pool, Pool, size). UNPATCHED @ 0x1C0006898: if (a1+size > MmUserProbeAddress || a1+size < a1) *(BYTE*)MmUserProbeAddress = 0; CallInProgressCompleted(a1, a1, size) on the raw user pointer.
security_findings.assembly:
PATCHED KsecIoctlFreeVm @ 0x18000E168:
0x18000E184  mov edx, 10h            ; Length
0x18000E192  lea r8d, [rdx-8]        ; Alignment = 8
0x18000E196  call cs:__imp_ProbeForRead
0x18000E1A2  movups xmm0, [rbx]      ; deref after probe

summary: The ksecdd.sys patch delivers input-validation hardening, not the high-severity exploit chains a first pass might infer. Two genuine, address-verified change classes are present. (1) NULL-pointer / dispatch hardening in the SSPI message and context APIs (EncryptMessage=SealMessage, DecryptMessage=UnsealMessage, QuerySecurityContextToken, ImpersonateSecurityContext, and the internal helpers DeleteUserModeContext and ProcessSecurityContextUnMarshalResponse): after the existing unsigned upper-bound check on the package index, the patch adds an lfence, reloads the dispatch-table base, and NULL-checks the package entry and function pointer before the CFG-guarded indirect call; the four entry points also gain NULL checks on the caller context handle (and message buffer), closing a local NULL-pointer dereference (BSOD). (2) Missing ProbeForRead on user-supplied IOCTL input buffers across the KsecIoctl* fast-I/O handler family (0 ProbeForRead calls unpatched vs 24 patched), with KsecIoctlHandleFunctionReturn and KsecIoctlClientCallback additionally converted from raw-user-pointer use to ProbeForRead + RtlCopyFromUser kernel-copy (double-fetch/TOCTOU fix). Corrections to note: the SSPI dispatch functions are named EncryptMessage/DecryptMessage (SSPI Seal/Unseal aliases); the indirect calls use Control-Flow-Guard (__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr), not retpolines; the claimed IOCTL 0x398000 handle leak and IOCTL 0x390058 arbitrary-VM-free fixes do not exist - both handlers are functionally identical in the two builds (0x478 is PROCESS_* rights on the caller's own process, the handle is kept in kernel state, and the handler is not removed); the ProbeForRead/ObReferenceObjectByHandle hardening the report attributed to KsecDispatch actually lives in the separate KsecIoctl* fast-I/O handlers; the SspiAcceptSecurityContextAsync cBuffers <= 0x12 check is a defensive bound on a caller output descriptor (error 0x8009035D at 0x1800241E4), not a network overflow fix. Overall severity: low for the NULL-deref/dispatch hardening (local DoS), medium for the missing-ProbeForRead / double-fetch family. No demonstrable OOB write, information leak, privilege escalation, or code-execution primitive.
